item.title: Trump’s understudy faces a fascinating audition
item.description: The vice president is a gifted communicator, but even he can’t sell a bad deal to three audiences.

item.title: Israel sees a grave strategic mistake unfolding in Iran
item.description: A kid-glove approach to Islamism, replete with sanctions relief for Tehran, marks the triumph of hope over experience.

item.title: He wants to rent out what he owns. It could cost him up to $10,000 a day.
item.description: Local government should be lowering barriers for entrepreneurs, not erecting more.

item.title: $0 co-pays could save billions
item.description: Bringing down drug costs is the first step to improve health care affordability.

item.title: For Albania’s Gen Z, Jared Kushner’s resort was the last straw
item.description: Armed with memes and a flamingo mascot, young Albanians have left Prime Minister Edi Rama without his usual last word.

item.title: Trump might really drain the swamp
item.description: The contractor in chief turned the National Mall into a costly mess.

item.title: ‘Teen takeovers’ spread fear. Here’s how to stop them.
item.description: Better lighting and open parks do more to stop flash mobs than jail.

item.title: Modern dating is miserable. Can it be fixed?
item.description: Host Megan McArdle talks with culture writer Kat Rosenfield about how dating apps, therapy-speak and online gender wars have changed romance — making flirtation feel risky, rejection feel catastrophic and desire feel suspect. They discuss the lost art of approaching people in real life, what older courtship norms got right and Rosenfield’s new novel, “How to Survive in the Woods.”

item.title: RFK Jr. has not abandoned his anti-vaccine agenda
item.description: The administration has quieted its efforts to blunt access to immunizations, but they continue nonetheless.

item.title: Did Trump achieve his goals in Iran?
item.description: What would a new Iran deal mean for the United States? In this episode of “Make It Make Sense,” Carine Hajjar sits down with Behnam Ben Taleblu, senior director of the Foundation for Defense of Democracies’ Iran program. They discuss whether a new agreement would strengthen or appease the Iranian regime, how Lebanon factors into the negotiations and what the ultimate costs and benefits could be for the U.S

item.title: Why I’m celebrating Anthony Bourdain’s birthday this week
item.description: The restaurant worker’s philosopher saw the misfits behind the machine.

item.title: Don’t quit this whole-brain workout
item.description: A culture that doesn’t wrestle its thoughts into structured prose might find it has fewer thoughts worth wrestling.

item.title: Ban phones in schools? That misses the bigger problem.
item.description: Only a small portion of phone use happens in class. What about the rest of kids’ lives?

item.title: An opening at Downing Street
item.description: Michael Ramirez cartoon on U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer resignation.

item.title: ‘We can’t stand Starmer’: Where it all went wrong for Britain’s prime minister
item.description: Economic gloom, defense cuts and scandal took down Britain’s prime minister.

item.title: Bowser’s warning to Janeese Lewis George on D.C. fiscal cliff
item.description: The bills may come due sooner than D.C.’s next mayor anticipates.
